text: After completing the fill layer, chip the slag and clean the toe lines thoroughly.
Add one more layer of fill using three 32nd inch E7018 electrodes.
Using the same technique, work angles and machine settings as used before.
The finished layer should be flat to slightly convex with complete fusion into both bevels.
It should be evenly distributed with an even fill from one end of the work piece to the
other.
Switch to 1 8th inch E7018 electrodes for the remainder of the joint and increase the
amperage.
Allow the work piece to cool briefly or switch to a second work piece.
Then deposit the remaining fill passes using the same technique and angles.
The final fill layer should fill the joint to within 1 16th of an inch from the surface
of the base metal.
For the cover pass, use the same electrode angles and Z-weave technique that you used
for the previous fill layers.
Melt into the edges of the joint by about 1 16th of an inch to ensure complete fusion
and pause at the toe lines to fill any undercut.
The finished weld should have a smooth contour and shall be a minimum of flush with the base
metal to a maximum of 1 8th inch reinforcement with no edge weld undercut or weld bead overlap
and with complete fusion into the sides of the joint.
Also, the joint must be filled out completely from one end of the work piece to the other.
The finish should be smooth and smooth.
The finish should be smooth and smooth.
The finish should be smooth and smooth.
The finish should be smooth and smooth.
As your welding skill improves, you have the option of switching to 5 32nd inch E7018 electrodes
for the fourth and remaining layers.
Remember to increase the average, then continue practice.
